mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change vs 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a late-cycle tightening labor market, raising fears of wage inflation and monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: After UNRATE drops below 4.5%, the proportion of positive 60-day SPX returns exceeds 55% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and industrial production reflect strong consumer demand and supply-side capacity, which together boost corporate profits and drive positive S&P 500 returns within a week.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and INDPRO are rising month-over-month, the median 5-day SPX return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a high inflation regime (CPI year-over-year above 4%), a rising federal funds rate compounds tightening expectations and increases equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increasing by at least 25bp over 3 months
failure_condition: In high-inflation periods with a fed funds hike, the average 60-day SPX return is positive, or the signal hit rate is below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising real GDP signals broad economic strength and higher future earnings, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a multi-month horizon as earnings revisions catch up.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of GDPC1 quarterly growth vs subsequent 60-day SPX return is below 0.03 over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surging above 1.5 million units indicate a booming residential construction sector and spillover demand, lifting equities positively over the following month.
failure_condition: After HOUST exceeds 1500, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns is not above 60% over any 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in payroll employment and industrial production reflects a synchronized labor and output expansion, reinforcing each other and boosting equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS and INDPRO both increase month-over-month, the average 20-day SPX return is not positive or the strategy Sharpe ratio is less than 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market (unemployment below 4%), rising interest rates aimed at cooling demand compress equity valuations and delay capex, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In the regime with UNRATE < 4% and a rate hike, the proportion of negative 60-day SPX returns is below 55% or the average return is positive.
